ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Reece Halpern appeared by zoom for and with Gregory Lewis (Administrator) by zoom.

The Court reminded Counsel that he should have gotten Court approval under Local Rule 14.92 before having the Personal Representative act as the Real Estate Agent and noted that the commission was 1%. The Court granted the Amended First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Reece R. Halpern for and with Gregory Lewis (Petitioner);
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court stated that it runs into the presumption that the decedent had the will at the time of death since the original can't be found and that the additional information according to the bookkeeper indicated that he wanted it to go to his ex-wife. Mr. Halpern stated that the testators will was last in his possession, that the drafting attorney would have hung on to the will, that the decedent gave Mr. Lewis a copy of the will, that the decedent had no intention of destroying the will, and that he retained a close relationship with Virginia David. The Court addressed revoking the prior will and writing a new one. Mr. Halpern stated that the ex-wife and ex father-in-law are consistent with the decedent's overall intent, that Ellen (the decedent's ex-wife) would not be the beneficiary it would be her father, and that the decedent continued an affection for his ex-wife even after the divorce. In addition, Mr. Halpern stated that the decedent had a life insurance policy that his ex-wife reaped the benefits of and that doesn't indicate an intent to revoke the will, that the bookkeeper found no evidence that the decedent was looking for a new attorney, that the decedent was a shut-in/phobic/bedridden, and that it was highly unlikely that he left his home to get a new will. Finally, Mr. Halpern stated that the decedent's brother, Dennis Quan, wants nothing to do with this estate. Discussions regarding Dennis Quan's declination, estrangement from his brother, and continuing the matter for a declaration from Dennis Quan are held. Mr. Halpern stated that Gregory Lewis' conversations with the decedent's brother reflect his wishes. The Court addressed Gregory Lewis' declaration stating that it talks about events going back quite some time not recent events. The Court noted that the decedent's brother has received notice and hasn't filed anything. Mr. Lewis stated that he had a number of conversations with the decedent's brother and that he wants the decedent's ex-wife to have everything since she suffered through the marriage for years, that he is aware that will doesn't give anything to the ex-wife, and that he knows it will go the ex-wife's father. The Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Continued To Oct-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Reece Halpern for and with Gregory Lewis.

Mr. Halpern stated that the Decedent was a hoarder, and that Mr. Lewis has made some effort, but is unable to complete the process without authority. Mr. Lewis stated that he looked at every accessible file and that one file cabinet is placed in the middle of the lower unit behind fifteen feet of stuff. The Court stated that there is a statutory presumption that when the will is not found and that original will remained in the possession of the Testator, if it can't be found, he has chosen to destroy it. Mr. Halpern stated that he received a declaration from the Decedent's Bookkeeper yesterday, that he filed it, that he believes the declaration helps them understand the Decedent's intent, and that the Ex-Wife remained the Beneficiary of the life insurance and she just received the proceeds. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any other evidence is to be filed as least four days before the hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Sep-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
